```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  lambda <- sample(seq(0.5,4,0.5),1); t <- sample(seq(0.5,3,0.5),1)
  ans <- lambda/(lambda+t)
  vals <- round(c(ans,t/(lambda+t),lambda/t,1/(lambda+t),exp(-lambda*t)),4)
  if (length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.4f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Se $X$ tem densidade exponencial $f(x)=\lambda e^{-\lambda x}$, $x>0$, com $\lambda=`r lambda`$, qual é $E(e^{-`r t`X})$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
Pela regra de transformação dentro da esperança,
$$E(e^{-tX})=\int_0^\infty e^{-tx}\lambda e^{-\lambda x}dx
=\frac{\lambda}{\lambda+t}
=`r sprintf("%.4f",ans)`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S8Q7_LOTUS_exponencial
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
